seoTitle: Crawley vs Crooked Brook — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Crawley and Crooked Brook. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Crawley vs Crooked Brook: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Crawley and Crooked Brook.
heading: Crawley vs Crooked Brook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Crawley, Western Australia and Crooked Brook, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crawley has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Crooked Brook (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Crawley or Crooked Brook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Crawley has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Crooked Brook scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Crawley scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Crooked Brook scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Crawley or Crooked Brook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crawley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
